compiled_state.title: As a developer, validate-work staging tests use @playwright/cli for browser interaction
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Migrate the validate-work skill's staging environment testing (Sub-agent 5) and the staging-environment-test.md prompt from Playwright MCP tools to @playwright/cli Bash commands. Update the playwright-verification.md prompt in debug-everything to use the same CLI patterns.
**Why**: validate-work dispatches a sub-agent for staging testing — exactly the context where MCP is unreliable. CLI migration ensures staging verification produces real results instead of hallucinated ones.
**Scope**:
- IN: validate-work/SKILL.md Sub-agent 5 browser references; staging-environment-test.md prompt; playwright-verification.md prompt; pre-flight check; output validation; session naming
- OUT: Changes to validate-work's non-browser sub-agents (1-4, 6)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, validate-work's staging browser testing uses @playwright/cli Bash commands with no references to Playwright MCP tools
  ← Satisfies: "All 5 skills use @playwright/cli commands via Bash instead of MCP tools"
- When this story is complete, each browser command in staging tests completes within 60 seconds
  ← Satisfies: "Each browser interaction command completes within the ~73s Bash tool timeout"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Session names must include worktree identifier
- [Security] Staging auth credentials in CLI session must be cleaned up after testing
- [Testing] Output validation guards needed for screenshot and snapshot artifacts

comments.body: Done definition update: When this story is complete, staging-environment-test.md prompt uses CLI command names (not MCP tool names like browser_run_code) throughout.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Consideration: [Consumer Impact] playwright-verification.md (in debug-everything/prompts/) contains MCP tool references and a hard path to PLAYWRIGHT-MCP-GUIDE.md — this cross-skill file must be updated as part of this story's scope.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Story complete: all tasks closed, CLI migration verified
comments.author: Test
